channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Edmonton councillor proposes renaming street to 'Forever Canadian' to promote unity
item.description: An Edmonton city councillor, Michael Janz, has proposed renaming a portion of 99th Avenue near the Alberta legislature to 'Forever Canadian Avenue' as a way to promote patriotism and support for Canad...

item.title: GeLex and FranSeth impress audiences with their performances on ASAP XP
item.description: On the latest episode of ASAP XP, the young Filipino stars GeLex and FranSeth displayed their remarkable chemistry as on-screen tandems. Fans were treated to energetic performances, showcasing both th...

item.title: Trump-Xi Summit: Working Towards Managed Stability in US-China Relations
item.description: The recent summit between President Donald Trump and President Xi Jinping in Beijing was largely ceremonial but carried underlying strategic importance. The meeting was primarily aimed at managing sup...

item.title: Rising Cross-Border Terrorism and Pakistan-Afghanistan Tensions
item.description: After months of declining terrorist incidents in March and April, Pakistan has witnessed a worrying surge in attacks originating from Afghan territory. From May 9 onwards, terrorists targeted police a...

item.title: Pedro Pascal surprises Disneyland visitors as the Mandalorian ahead of new film release
item.description: Pedro Pascal, known for his role as the Mandalorian in the Disney+ series, made a surprise appearance at Disneyland on Saturday, delighting fans aboard the Millennium Falcon: Smuggler’s Run ride. Dres...

item.title: NDLEA Seizes Drugs Concealed in Clothes, Garri and Farmlands Across Nigeria
item.description: Operatives of the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) don intercept plenty drugs wey dem hide for different places like cartons of clothes, garri, and farmlands across Nigeria. For Lagos, dem...

item.title: International University of Management to Launch 120-Bed Hospital and Training Facility Near Ondangwa by 2027
item.description: The International University of Management (IUM), led by founder and chairperson David Namwandi, announced plans to establish a 120-bed private hospital near Ondangwa, expected to open by mid-2027. Th...

item.title: Rising Hypertension Cases Among Adults Under 45 in Gauteng Prompt Health Alerts
item.description: The Gauteng Department of Health has raised concerns over a growing number of adults under 45 being diagnosed with hypertension, often referred to as the “silent killer”. During the 2025/26 financial ...

item.title: John Tongshinen defeats Yusuf Gagdi in Plateau APC primary election
item.description: For Plateau State, na big surprise don happen as Yusuf Gagdi, wey be one of the popular federal lawmakers and chairman of the House of Representatives Committee on Navy, lose him seat for the APC prim...

item.title: Angus Taylor's Proposal to Limit Welfare Access for Permanent Residents Sparks Debate
item.description: Angus Taylor has unveiled a policy that would deny government support to over 1.2 million permanent residents who are not Australian citizens, regardless of how long they have contributed to taxes. Th...

item.title: Ramaphosa plans court challenge against parliamentary impeachment process
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa is set to challenge the establishment of a parliamentary committee tasked with investigating his possible impeachment over the Phala Phala farm scandal. Following a Constitut...

item.title: Pakistan to Enhance NAB Transparency and Strengthen Anti-Corruption Efforts Under IMF Guidance
item.description: ISLAMABAD – In line with IMF’s growing emphasis on tackling corruption directly, Pakistan has been directed to make the National Accountability Bureau (NAB) more autonomous and transparent. According ...

item.title: Analyst Highlights JD Vance’s Challenges in Gaining Influence Within MAGA Movement
item.description: JD Vance, currently serving as U.S. Vice President, has been considered a potential successor to former President Donald Trump within the MAGA political movement. However, recent analysis suggests his...

item.title: DOH Coordinates with WHO Following Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda
item.description: The Department of Health (DOH) of the Philippines has announced active coordination with the World Health Organization (WHO) following the declaration of an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic o...

item.title: Candid Photos Reveal Daily Life of an American Family in 1960
item.description: The early 1960s in the United States represented a transitional period between the structured domestic life of the 1950s and the transformative cultural changes of the later decade. This article showc...

item.title: WHO Declares Public Health Emergency Amid Ebola Outbreak in DRC and Uganda
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has declared a public health emergency due to an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) and Uganda. As of May 17, 2026, at least 80 suspected deat...

item.title: Seven Arrested in Atlantis After Murder and Hijacking of Two Women
item.description: Seven suspects, aged between 20 and 40, have been arrested in connection with the hijacking and murder of two women in Atlantis. The victims, both foreign nationals from Ghana living in South Africa, ...

item.title: Australian sprinter Eddie Nketia posts impressive 100m time in the US
item.description: Australian sprinter Eddie Nketia has once again drawn attention with his latest 100-metre sprint performance in the United States. Known for his explosive speed and technique, Nketia continues to make...

item.title: Cornet Mamabolo Reflects on Portraying Peter Mokaba and His First Executive Producer Role
item.description: Cornet Mamabolo, formerly known for his role as Thabo 'T-Bose' Maputla on Skeem Saam, has taken on a significant new challenge portraying radical youth leader Peter Mokaba in the film 'Studying Under ...

item.title: Cecil Beaton’s 1951 Vogue Portraits Capture Jackie and Lee Bouvier’s High-Society Debut
item.description: In 1951, renowned photographer Cecil Beaton created a memorable series of portraits featuring the Bouvier sisters—Jacqueline Bouvier, later known as Jackie Kennedy Onassis, and her younger sister Lee,...
